evidence_quotes.quote: Most of the acts the game companies make is agains their own EULA and terms of conditions

paragraphs.text: The core issue in the gaming industry is that players who purchase digital games or online products can have their access unfairly revoked when servers are shut down, licenses are terminated or quite frankly sometimes even when developers just feel like it. This undermines consumer rights, as buyers lose access to products they legally own without fair recourse or alternatives, such as offline modes or server emulation. Most of the acts the game companies make is agains their own EULA and terms of conditions, but as a citizens we cannot fight agains a massive corporation. I urge regulators and industry leaders to implement policies ensuring long-term access to purchased games, preserving both consumer trust and gaming history where appopriate.
